review_title: A Reliable All-in-One Tool for Modern API Development
description: My overall experience with Postman has been very positive. After more than three years of regular use, it has proven to be a reliable and efficient tool for developing and testing multiple REST APIs, and more recently, a GraphQL API. Its consistency, versatility, and maturity make it a dependable part of my daily development workflow.
author: Jamsly M.
pros: Postman is an effective all-in-one tool for API development, supporting REST, GraphQL, and gRPC in a single interface. It streamlines the entire API workflow from building and testing requests to documentation and automation without requiring multiple tools. Environment management, scripting, and collaboration features make it efficient for both individual developers and teams.
cons: The main limitation in for me is the lack of a fully functional offline mode. Many features depend on an active internet connection, which can be restrictive in low-connectivity environments or when working offline.

review_title: An Essential Tool for Any Developer Working with APIs
description: Extremely positive. It’s one of those tools that feels indispensable once you’re used to it. Despite some performance quirks, it’s the gold standard for API work for a reason. It just works.
author: Tyler V.
pros: It completely eliminates the guesswork in API development. I can test endpoints, debug responses, and automate entire workflows without ever leaving the app. The collaboration features are a lifesaver – sharing a collection with a teammate is instant, and everyone always has the latest version. It’s made working with APIs faster and way more reliable.
cons: The app can be a serious memory hog, especially with large workspace or long-running sessions. I also miss the simplicity of the older versions sometimes; the constant push toward the web app and new features can make it feel bloated. The learning curve for advanced scripting is also pretty steep.

review_title: Useful for API and Integration tests
description: Overall, it has saved our team a lot of time. AI is becoming more and more of a substitute for Postman now, but at least for the next little while, it will continue to be useful for API and integration testing.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Postman was easy to install and convenient with its Team option. We often used a shared harness to hit our backend and do api testing
cons: There was some flakiness with retrieving jwt auth tokens (client credentials as well). Often times we would need to manually reset the tokens.

review_title: FRUSTRATING, Mandatory registration !
description: FRUSTRATING experience ! I would never ever recommend anyone to create an account to test something as simple as API. My usage is about once a month in last 12 months and forcing me to login is something I DO NOT WANT. Since they DO not have any feedback system, I am wasting so time to find a place to send them a message. POSTMAN used to be great please remove mandatory registration.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is a good software, provides quick results for testing APIs and debugging things. Somewhat easy to use. I didn't need to read any documentation to use it.
cons: FORCED ACCOUNT CREATION ! For testing a simple API they want to track your account. ONE OF THE WORST USER EXPERIENCE !!!!! I have been looking for a replacement for a long long time.

review_title: Effortless API Testing tool
description: Overall, Postman provides a positive experience with its user-friendly design, efficient API testing features, and robust capabilities, despite a slight learning curve for beginners.
author: Parsa G.
pros: Postman simplifies API testing with an intuitive interface, easy JSON data retrieval, and integrated testing capabilities.
cons: The interface can sometimes be confusing, especially for beginners, leading to a steeper learning curve when trying to navigate and utilize all features effectively.

review_title: API Endpoints Testing tool for developers
description: I have been using Postman for two years, and it has helped me test the API's before using them in the application. The results have helped me to analyze and then modify my code if required depending on the requirements.
author: Bashir A.
pros: The setup for postman is very simple, and it is also available on various platforms like the desktop application or a website. Data could be sent to the server in different formats like Text, JSON, form-data, etc. Collaboration with other developers is also possible in Postman.
cons: It can’t have HTML page viewer in the results along with the HTML code. Apart from this, I find Postman as a very useful tool for testing API's.

review_title: Postman our choice for API testing and collaboration
description: Our dev and QA groups now work on the same system. This cut integration bugs by 50% and onboarding of new engineers became faster everything is documented and you can share.
author: Vittal B.
pros: It's the heart and soul of our development. From writing test suites, to simulating endpoints to sharing collections with QA, it’s a game changer for how rapid we can iterate our APIs.
cons: It is a bit resource heavy on the system and the workspace permissions can be a bit restrictive toward cross team visibility.

review_title: Budding dev finds it useful
description: Used on and off for about 7 years, from testing other people's end points, testing integrations and starting my own journey in developement.
author: Stuart P.
pros: As a budding developer I can try my endpoints without having to build any UI, saving me time and effort.
cons: Sometimes authentication can be a little frustrating for a beginner - bearer token implementation especially.

review_title: Postman : A Powerful API testing software
description: Overall I like to use Postman to test my APIs and it is really helpful to write some scripts and Automate the process.
author: Parth S.
pros: The Postman UI is very user-friendly and easy to adapt for new learners. It is the best application for performing all kinds of API testing.
cons: Integrating with other applications and creating automation tools is more difficult than other API testing software.

review_title: Great tool
author: Linze T.
pros: I like that you can save workspaces with method submissions and different header values or authorisation keys. It returns values very quickly and formats documents nicely.
cons: Bit of a learning curve with customising everything to save or setting up workspaces efficiently but nothing too major.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Good tool for creating ad-hoc http requests
author: Eric C.
pros: Essential tool for ad-hoc testing of backend endpoints. Complete feature set, robust and reliable.
cons: Scratchpad has been removed and now requires an account to use. Backs up all the requests/collections/credentials to their servers!

review_title: Review of postman
author: Vaja H.
pros: It provides user friendly interface and provides integration support for different CI/CD pipelines
cons: It doesn not provide much support for debugging

review_title: Essential Tool for Anyone in Software Development
author: Gabriele O.
pros: Most essential API management tools out there.
cons: No complaints about it. Absolutely an essential tool for any Software Developer or Engineer.

review_title: Postman does everything a tool for API development needs to do and more!
description: Postman is my tool of choice when working with APIs. It does offer most of the features one may need while also being easy to use and learn.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I am using the free version of Postman in my daily work as a software tester. It's the application I use for working with APIs and, for me, it offers most of the features I need while also being intuitive and having a pleasant UI. Regarding REST services, it does offer the functionalities anyone would expect for running the APIs, nothing special here - it does what a software used for running webservices should do. Postman stands out for me for the additional features it offers - posibilities to add multiple environments and variables based on those environments, a history tab with the requests and responses ran in the past, ability to write tests in app, ability to automatically run those tests (with some limitations for the free version), grouping collections under a workspace and then change through workspaces (in case there are multiple different projects a user has to work with), ability to export and share the collection and environment variables and possibility to run the tests within a CI system. Even though I barely used it for other types of services, I know that Postman works with SOAP and GraphQL too.
cons: I would say that Postman is in a weird place with the "Team Workspaces" for the free version. For this, they offer 25 different calls that can be registered within the team. In every situation I was in, this number was too low, even for the smallest projects I worked with. It was never useful to start working within a team workspace because we were always getting to the point where the older calls were deleted because we were exceeding the limit. After a few tries, I gave up completely on this feature. Personally, a limit to 50 would have been more useful - I can understand this is their business model and they offer payed alternatives, but in my opinion the "Team Workspace" feature is not usable in the state which is now.

review_title: API test, sharing and release made easy.
description: It pretty well does it's basic an expected job very well and make it very convenient to developer fraternity to rely upon. So, generally it terms out to share the Postman collection sharing in development lifecycle instead any API document or anything and that pretty much makes life easier to follow and adopt.
author: Gupteshwar J.
pros: It's ease of use and impact, without a end application/consumer in sight we can design, develop, test and make it ready for the various planned usage across platforms and use cases etc..Very much makes it close to 99.99% development and still some issues may arise in actual but either not at all or very minimal issues or so. So, makes development pretty much independent.
cons: Nothing much as it does it's basic job very well. Very much skirting issues like CORS or such request failures which occurs in real life but can't be reproduced with Postman anytime.

review_title: The most versatile and powerful tool when working with APIs
author: Krunal B.
pros: 1) Postman has a user-friendly interface and a simple, intuitive design, making it easy for users to get started with API development and testing.2) Postman supports team collaboration, allowing multiple team members to work on the same API project and share their work with others.3) Postman provides a wide range of testing and monitoring tools, allowing users to test and validate their APIs to ensure they are working as expected.4) Postman integrates with security tools, providing an extra layer of security for APIs and ensuring that sensitive data is protected.
cons: While Postman has a user-friendly interface, it can still be complex for users who are new to API development and testing.

review_title: API Testing Tool
description: Honestly saying, I am using postman since 2+ years, I first time used postman during my internship period and I was amazed to see the features and functionality provided by postman, if there is no backend data available and you want to work on front end then using postman you can easily test APIs whether they are working or not and can go ahead for further development. Also postman saves previously used APIs so that later we can use them if we want. It is really easy to use and it saves a lot of time of developers.Postman is a crucial part in every backend and front end developer's life.
author: Sakshi R.
pros: Postman is a fantastic tool for API testing, generally every developer needs to test API before creating an application,I would say postman is easy to use and it is user friendly tool, User only need to paste API link and can see the data belongs to that API. Postman allows various methods like GET, PUT,POST etc...Also if you paste the API link postman allows you to see the code in JSON, XML, python and many more. Postman allows to document the api data in collection and later can share with team members. User can save the APIs as collections and can easily use later directly from collections.
cons: I am generally using postman to test APIs but sometimes I felt that when I paste the link of API in postman and hit enter it is not showing any response or any data but when I close the postman and try again it works, that's the only thing which I don't like else postman is a best friend for every developer.

review_title: Best HTTP client in the market.
description: I've used postman every day for the past 2 to 3 years as a tool to test my services, specially on edge case scenarios that were not covered by the initial unit tests, or that are discovered by the QA team. Postman being so intuitive has allowed me to be more effective at my job and, often times, discard the posibility of the error being in the backend without much effort. It would not be an understatement to say this software has saved me at least 100 hours of work. Would recommend it to anyone working primarily on the backend.
author: Orlando P.
pros: I've got almost no complaints on postman, specially on their installable desktop platforms. I can completely rely on it and trust their functionality in order to trust the services I work with every day. The configuration options for all requests are perfect and the supported options and exchange formats is up to date with industry standards. Plus. With an unexistent barrier of entry on the free tier, there is no better http client out there. I'd even pick it above Curl.
cons: If I had one minor pet peeve is that the web client is not as intuitive nor complete as the others, often failing to save my changes. However this can be very easily side stepped by just downloading the correct client for your OS, so I hardly count it as a negative.

review_title: Powerful tool for API development and testing
description: I use Postman to develop, test and monitor different API. For beginning it was not so easy to learn, but now I can efficiently use it to complete my tasks
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like friendly interface, rich features, useful response visualization, testing and monitoring. If I have question, I always can find answer in Postman community
cons: For very complex API sometimes there are performance issues. Also for new users it may be a little hard to learn and understand how to use full power of Postman.

review_title: I am constantly finding new things that I can do with the application
description: I love using Postman because if does exactly what I want it to do. I work with enough people that we are always finding new features and seeing how much more we can use this service for our company.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Where I work we have to take a lot of different language formats (HTML, Javascript, JSON and so forth) my preference is XML and not only will it convert the Language format, it also has an option to "Beautify" As long as you know what you are doing, Postman executes REST API's perfectly. You can set up your parameters, headers, text Bodies and even set up authorizations. I have dozens of calls saved so that it was remarkably easy to just run the API and it is good to go in seconds.
cons: There are two things that I have found to be a challenge : 1. With large Response file bytes that come through, if can take a really long time to load and interact with the data. Postman can become sluggish and even non-responsive for longer periods of time. The fix is to set the bodies data to RAW and then it will run smooth, but by default, this option is not selected. 2. If you do not know what you are doing - this can feel not very user-friendly. There is a training section called "Bootcamp" but it told you what to do rather than what things are for. An example I have was I was trying to figure out what all of the different Methods produce but it would just tell me that the most common method is "GET" Just know that the training is more of a walkthrough rather than a roadmap.

review_title: Best API building and testing tool I've used so far.
description: Postman helps in my day to day tasks of development and testing. I usually do all my end to end testing using postman. It also saves all the logs and history so I don't have to worry about shutting down my pc abruptly.
author: Divyanshu A.
pros: Postman is the best tool out there to test the APIs. I have been using it for a few years now and all my colleagues use this too. Best thing about this tool is that you can save all the Endpoints and don't have to enter them repeatedly.
cons: One thing it lacks is that you have to import the whole collection if you have several APIs why in other applications you can just open it via link. But apart from that it is well and good.

review_title: Must have this tool to develop and test API in real time.
description: From my observations and knowledge, Postman is a well-designed and reliable tool that offers a range of features for API development, testing, and automation. Its intuitive user interface makes it easy to use, even for those with limited coding experience, while its powerful collaboration capabilities allow for faster feedback and testing.
author: Rajesh R.
pros: One of the most significant advantages of Postman is its user-friendly interface. The platform is intuitive and straightforward to use, even for those without extensive coding experience. It allows you to quickly create and test your APIs, with a variety of features to help you streamline the process.Overall, Postman is an essential tool for any developer working with APIs. It is easy to use, highly customizable and features powerful collaboration capabilities. If you're looking for a reliable and efficient way to develop, test, and share APIs, Postman is an excellent choice.
cons: Paid features: While Postman offers a free version, many of its more advanced features are only available with a paid subscription. This may be a consideration for individual developers or smaller teams who are working with limited budgets
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Best for testing my Apis
description: Overall Postman is best for me and fulfills every requirement for testing my APIs.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The thing that I like the most about postman is that it's easy to use and I can always get a curl request from my colleague and easily import that API request to my Postman account and save it for the future. I also like to make a collection of API requests that I regularly use. and I also keep my hostnames in variables so I don't have to type the full hostname whenever I have to make a new API request.
cons: Everything is good but It consumes too much RAM while running. other than that everything is good.

review_title: Postman - the best with REST APIs
description: As i am using the free Postman version (incidental use, not day to day use) i am probably scratching the service as to the functionality of Postman. I use both the desktop client and the browser based version. THe best about Postman is that you can simply jump into old projects and you will be up and running again in no time.
author: Rob B.
pros: Postman is a great tool for testing REST API, making connections to REST APIs. Although not as straight forward as SOAP UI (REST Projects), I think that the possibilities of Postman are great with regards to creating an storing projects that you are working on, including storing all kinds of values like API Keys, client IDs, client secrets and so on. Visiting a workspace that you worked in/on in the past allows you to pick up where you left of. Make sure that you save the aforementioned values and you have no issues picking up and refreshing for instance an OUATH2 token with a refresh token.
cons: As many developers dive into the software and do not read any 'how to' documents, finding your way is a little daunting. However, I managed to figure out the configurations and options without to much trouble. As often many options cloud the interface but with some puzzling you should be able to use this tool.

review_title: Postman Review
description: Overall I am very happy with the postman. It provides everything which helps to test the APIs and helps in the development of APIs. I have been using it from a long time and I have not found a single thing to complain about it.
author: Mohit R.
pros: Postman is a very useful tool for API testing. I have been using postman since my third year in college for testing the APIs created for the project and now I am using it in my job. Its user interface is very easy to use for beginners. Another feature that I like about postman is that it provides the functionality to collaborate with team members. And collection of APIs can also be created and that collection can be shared with other team members also.
cons: I have been using the postman from a long time and I have not found any flaws in it. It does its work perfectly without any problems and helps to test the APIs for the project.
